summary: Soundboard capture of a high‑energy Boston Garden show highlighted by a thunderous Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Fire on the Mountain and stellar second‑set jams, with generally good mix though Jerry could be louder.
review: The concert is praised for its powerful set two – especially the explosive Help / Slipknot / Fire sequence, a ripping Truckin', and soaring renditions of Watchtower, Morning Dew and Lovelight. Reviewers note the soundboard source provides clear audio, but the mix could use more Jerry in the front. Overall the performance is described as one of the band’s finest post‑reunion shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Strong musical praise (standout songs, monster playing) outweighs minor mix comments; limited to two reviews (one very brief) so confidence is low.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear overall sound but the mix leaves Jerry's guitar a bit buried; no major drop‑outs reported.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Fire on the Mountain
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds smoothly with strong openers; second set ramps up with a thunderous Help/Slipknot/Fire burst and maintains high energy through extended jams, ending on an uplifting Lovelight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for textbook‑perfect Jack-a-Roe and dialed‑in Morning Dew, though reviewers note his guitar should be louder in the mix.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as a 'monster' throughout the night, especially on bass during Help on the Way; reviewer wishes his level was turned up more.
band_member_comments.Bob: Commended for solid double‑stop runs, glorious Black Throated Wind, strong Masterpiece feel and confident Lovelight delivery.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (Bruce) adds a noticeable accordion/piano texture that some listeners found less appealing, but overall his presence is positive.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming highlighted as 'killer' with powerful ending jams leading into Watchtower and Morning Dew.
